LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GARY VARUM, ALEX VARUM, NATALIE KREIGEL, 1122 UNIVERSITY AVENUE, L.L.C, 1122 UNIVERSITY, LLC, GIA GROUP, LLC, CALIFORNIA FAMILY COMPANY, LLC AND IRINA VARUM'S DEMURRER TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. BOTH OF PLAINTIFF ARKADY BERGER'S CLAIMS ARE PREMISED ON ALLEGED FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS THAT WERE DONE TO DELAY OR IMPAIR MR. BERGER'S EFFORTS TO COLLECT ON THE JUDGMENT IN HIS FAVOR. AS A MATTER OF LAW, ONCE THE JUDGMENT WAS FULLY SATISFIED, MR. BERGER'S CLAIMS FOR FRAUDULENT TRANSFER CEASED TO BE VIABLE. MR. BERGER CITES NO PUBLISHED AUTHORITY, AND THE COURT IS AWARE OF NONE, THAT PERMITS A JUDGMENT CREDITOR TO SEEK CONSEQUENTIAL AND/OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES RESULTING FROM THE DELAY IN PAYMENT OF A JUDGMENT DUE TO FRAUDULENT TRANSFERS THAT OCCURRED PRIOR TO SATISFACTION OF THE JUDGMENT. ALTHOUGH MR. BERGER CONTENDS THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO SEEK "REGULAR FRAUD DAMAGES," HE HAS NOT ALLEGED, NOR HAS HE SHOWN THAT HE CAN ALLEGE A "REGULAR FRAUD" CLAIM. BECAUSE MR. BERGER HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THERE IS ANY REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT HE WILL BE ABLE TO AMEND HIS COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CLAIM, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer McGlone of Keller Sloan Roman Holland LLP telephone number 415-249-8330 for Plaintiff Arkady Berger.
Adam Polakoff of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS GARY VARUM, ALEX VARUM, NATALIE KREIGEL, 1122 UNIVERSITY AVENUE, L.L.C, 1122 UNIVERSITY, LLC, GIA GROUP, LLC, CALIFORNIA FAMILY COMPANY, LLC AND IRINA VARUM'S demurrer to both causes of action in the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. Both of plaintiff Arkady Berger's claims are premised on alleged fraudulent transfers that were done to delay or impair Mr. Berger's efforts to collect on the judgment in his favor. As a matter of law, once the judgment was fully satisfied, Mr. Berger's claims for fraudulent transfer ceased to be viable. Mr. Berger cites no published authority, and the court is aware of none, that permits a judgment creditor to seek consequential and/or punitive damages resulting from the delay in payment of a judgment due to fraudulent transfers that occurred prior to satisfaction of the judgment. Although Mr. Berger contends that he is entitled to seek "regular fraud damages," he has not alleged, nor has he shown that he can allege a "regular fraud" claim. Because Mr. Berger has not shown that there is any reasonable possibility that he will be able to amend his complaint to allege a viable claim, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Precluding Or Limiting The Deposition Of Irina Varum Aka Irene Varum
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RONALD K. LOSCH, ESQ. of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel No. 415-956-8400 appearing for the Plaintiff Arkady Berger aka Alex Berger;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS GARY VARUM, ALEX VARUM, NATALIE KREIGEL, AND IRINA VARUM'S Motion For Protective Order Precluding Or Limiting The Deposition Of Irina Varum Aka Irene Varum, the Motion for Protective Order is DENIED. The deponent is a named defendant in this case. The doctor's letter submitted by the moving party is vague and does not establish that the deponent is completely unable to testify or that there are no accommodations that can be made to enable her to testify. The moving party's declarations consist of second-hand statements or statements made without sufficiently explaining the basis for the personal knowledge to make such statements, or improper opinion testimony. Counsel for the moving party did not engage in good faith meet and confer prior to filing this motion. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,625.00, payable within 10 days of notice of entry of order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ALEX VARUM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. In order for collateral estoppel to apply, the issue "must have been actually litigated in the former proceeding." (South Sutter LLC v. LJ Sutter Partners, L.P. (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 634, 661-662.) Although the Second Amended Complaint in the underlying action generally alleges at paragraph seven that Defendant Alex Varum is the alter ego of Gary Varum and Telesis, there is nothing in the judicially noticeable documents to indicate that this issue was "actually litigated." The tentative decision and final judgment are silent on the issue of alter ego as to Alex Varum. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
